items.headline: Gibson works 4 2/3 innings as the 1st Orioles starting pitcher to debut at Yankee Stadium
items.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — Long before the Orioles let another game against the New York Yankees slip away, Baltimore starter Trey Gibson enjoyed a memorable major league debut.
Gibson worked 4 2/3 innings Sunday and became the first Oriole starting pitcher to make his big league debut at Yankee Stadium since the franchise moved from St. Louis following the 1953 season.

items.headline: Cunningham and Harris help Pistons finish comeback for first playoff series win in 18 years
items.description:
DETROIT (AP) — Cade Cunningham had 32 points and 12 assists, Tobias Harris added 30 points and the Detroit Pistons beat the Orlando Magic 116-94 in Game 7 on Sunday to win a playoff series for the first time in 18 years.
Cunningham averaged 32.4 points for Detroit, which last won a postseason series by beating Orlando in the second round in 2008. The Pistons advance to play the winner of Sunday evening’s Game 7 between the Cleveland Cavaliers and the Toronto Raptors. Game 1 will be Tuesday at Little Caesars Arena.

items.headline: The Pittsburgh Pirates believe they're built differently this year. A sweep of Cincy offered proof
items.description:
PITTSBURGH (AP) — Paul Skenes stood at his locker in a near-silent Pittsburgh clubhouse on Thursday after a four-game home sweep at the hands of St. Louis and offered a prediction when asked if he was confident things wouldn't snowball on a Pirates team still finding its way.
“I think we'll be alright,” the reigning NL Cy Young winner offered.

items.headline: Gladbach sink Dortmund, St Pauli edge closer to drop
items.description: Borussia Moenchengladbach ensured they will remain in Germany's top flight next season when Haris Tabakovic scored with two minutes remaining to beat Borussia Dortmund on Sunday, while Mainz pushed...

items.headline: Talisman Endrick fires resurgent Lyon into third in France
items.description: Cash-strapped Lyon edged clear of a clutch of clubs vying for Ligue 1's last Champions League spot with a 4-2 win over Rennes on Sunday...

items.headline: Busch stars as the surging Cubs beat the Diamondbacks 8-4 for their 5th consecutive win
items.description:
CHICAGO (AP) — Michael Busch hit a three-run triple and a sacrifice fly, and the Chicago Cubs beat the Arizona Diamondbacks 8-4 on Sunday for their fifth straight win.
Moisés Ballesteros homered as Chicago improved to 15-3 in its last 18 games. Matthew Boyd (2-1) pitched six innings of two-run ball.

items.headline: Cam Smith gets go-ahead 2-run single in 10th; Astros hold off Red Sox 3-1
items.description:
BOSTON (AP) — Cam Smith hit a two-out, two-run single off the Green Monster in the 10th inning, and the Houston Astros beat the Boston Red Sox 3-1 on Sunday to take the series.
Jarren Duran hit a solo homer for the Red Sox, his second homer in three games after belting a three-run shot in Friday’s victory.

items.headline: Vinicius double delays Barca title as Real Madrid down Espanyol
items.description: Vinicius Junior struck twice for Real Madrid in a 2-0 win over Espanyol on Sunday to make La Liga leaders Barcelona wait...

items.headline: Max Verstappen takes 5th at Miami after a spin but still wants improvements to his car
items.description:
MIAMI GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — Max Verstappen was feeling a bit more comfortable in his car at the Miami Grand Prix, but the four-time Formula 1 champion still believes Red Bull has improvements to make.
After testing out Red Bull's upgrades made during a five-week break in the F1 schedule, Verstappen had his best result of the season on Sunday, finishing fifth despite spinning from second on the opening lap.
